# manygit

> A lazygit-style terminal UI for a whole tree of git repos. Point it at a
> directory and it shows every git repository nested under it on one screen — which
> are ahead, behind, or dirty — then fetches, pulls, pushes, and switches
> branches on the one under the cursor, without cd-ing between them.

manygit is a single Go binary for macOS and Linux, built with Bubble Tea and
Lip Gloss. It is free and open source at https://github.com/rabeeh-ta/manygit

## What problem it solves

If you keep more than a handful of git repos in one tree — microservices, a
workspace of packages, a folder of plugins, nested at whatever depth — answering
"which of these have uncommitted work, and which are behind origin?" means
`cd`-ing through every one, or maintaining a shell loop like:

    for d in */; do (cd "$d" && git status); done

manygit replaces that loop with a screen you can act from. It is to a folder of
repos what lazygit is to a single repo.

## Install

    curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rabeeh-ta/manygit/main/install.sh | bash

From source (needs Go 1.24+):

    git clone https://github.com/rabeeh-ta/manygit && cd manygit
    go build -o ~/.local/bin/manygit .

## Usage

    manygit                 # scan the current directory
    manygit --root ~/work   # scan a specific folder

It walks the folder three levels deep by default, skips `node_modules`, `vendor`,
`dist`, `target` and similar, and groups the repos it finds by parent directory.

## Panes

- `1` Repos — every repo, its current branch, and its status
- `2` Scripts — `*.sh` files near the root, runnable in place
- `3` Branches — the highlighted repo's local and remote branches
- `4` PRs — GitHub pull requests (needs the `gh` CLI, 2.12+)
- `5` Graph — a colored commit graph for the highlighted repo
- `6` Changes — changed files of the selected commit, with in-place diffs
- `7` Output — live output of the last script run

## Keys

Actions apply to the repo under the `>` cursor.

- `j` / `k` — move within the focused pane
- `←` / `→` — hop between Repos and that repo's Branches
- `enter` — open a repo's branches, check one out, run a script, or check out a PR
- `s` / `p` — sync (fetch then fast-forward pull) / push
- `d` / `D` — discard changes after a confirm (`D` also deletes untracked files)
- `f` / `r` — fetch this repo / refetch all
- `/` — filter the focused list; Repos match on name AND current branch (and the
  tag while `t` is on)
- `F` — show only repos that are changed, ahead, or behind
- `t` — show each repo's latest tag inline
- `g` / `n` — full-screen commit graph / full-screen news feed
- `m` — in the PRs pane, toggle your PRs and review requests
- `o` / `z` — open the repo in your editor / zoom the focused pane
- `?` — the keybindings and the status legend
- `,` — settings (theme, AI harness, news window, scan depth, glyphs, editor)
- `q` — quit

## Status column

- `ok` — up to date with upstream
- `↑N` — N commits ahead, something to push
- `↓N` — N commits behind, something to pull
- `↑N ↓M` — diverged
- `*N` — N files changed in the working tree
- `no-remote` — a local-only repo, never pushed anywhere
- `!` — the branch has no upstream, or git errored

## Other features

- GitHub PRs: with the `gh` CLI signed in, `4` lists your open PRs and the ones
  awaiting your review. `enter` checks the PR's branch out in the matching local
  clone via `gh pr checkout`.
- AI news feed: with `claude` or `codex` on PATH, the top bar summarizes recent
  commits across every repo in English. Cached four hours.
- Themes: `default`, `serika_dark`, `dracula`, `nord`, `catppuccin`, `8008`,
  adapted from monkeytype. Configurable in `~/.config/manygit/config.yml` or the
  `,` settings screen.

## Safety

manygit never writes to the folder you launch it from, and never force-pushes,
merges, or rebases. The only destructive action is discarding a repo's changes
(`d` / `D`), which always asks first.

## Links

- Site and interactive demo: https://rabeeh-ta.github.io/manygit/
- Source: https://github.com/rabeeh-ta/manygit
- Releases: https://github.com/rabeeh-ta/manygit/releases
- Issues: https://github.com/rabeeh-ta/manygit/issues
